hero.title: Official Policies of Makani Spot
hero.subtitle:
Privacy Policy · Terms & Conditions · Intellectual Property Rights
Last updated: May 20, 2026

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How we collect, protect, and use your data on the Makani Spot platform
privacy.intro: Makani Spot is the digital platform operated by Kiosq SpotHub, specializing in managing and leasing small commercial spaces, connecting space owners with small-business owners and bazaar organizers across Egypt. By using the platform, you agree to this Privacy Policy.

s1.title: 1 — Scope of the Policy and Operating Entity
s1.p: This policy applies to all services, platforms, and applications operated by Kiosq SpotHub, including:
s1.li1: The small commercial space listing and rental platform (Makani Spot)
s1.li3: The company's current and future applications
s1.li4: Services connecting space owners with tenants and business owners
s1.li5: Interactions of all user categories: space owners, tenants, bazaar organizers, and service providers

s2.title: 2 — Data We Collect
s2.personalValue: Name · Phone · Email · National ID (when required)
s2.businessValue: Business name · Commercial registration · Tax card · Licenses
s2.technicalValue: Device type · IP address · Browser · Cookies
s2.locationValue: Approximate or precise location to show nearby spaces
s2.p: We also collect content you upload: photos, logos, kiosk or space designs, business descriptions, and messages and interactions within the platform.

s3.title: 3 — How We Collect Your Data
s3.li1: Directly: when you create an account, upload content, book a space, or contact us.
s3.li2: Automatically: through cookies, analytics tools, and performance/security logs.
s3.li3: From third parties: electronic payment providers, identity verification providers, and approved service partners.

s4.title: 4 — Purposes for Using Your Data
s4.li1: Creating accounts and verifying user identity
s4.li2: Facilitating bookings, rentals, and communication between parties
s4.li3: Showing spaces and activities relevant to your needs
s4.li4: Operating the platform and improving service quality
s4.li5: Detecting fraud and maintaining platform security
s4.li6: Complying with Egyptian legal and regulatory requirements
s4.li7: Resolving disputes and enforcing the Terms and Conditions

s5.title: 5 — Legal Basis for Processing
s5.p: Your data is processed based on: your explicit consent, performance of contractual obligations between us, compliance with Egyptian law, or the company's legitimate interests in a manner that does not conflict with your rights — in accordance with Egyptian Personal Data Protection Law No. 151 of 2020.

s6.title: 6 — Sharing Your Data with Third Parties
s6.p: We do not sell your data. We may share it only in the following cases:
s6.li1: With space owners or tenants to facilitate agreed bookings
s6.li2: With government or judicial authorities when legally required
s6.li3: With service providers (cloud hosting, payment gateways) under strict confidentiality agreements

s7.title: 7 — Data Protection and Security
s7.p: Kiosq SpotHub is committed to implementing security measures including encryption in transit and at rest, access-control systems, and continuous system monitoring. However, absolute protection against all breaches beyond the company's reasonable control cannot be guaranteed.

s8.title: 8 — User Rights
s8.right1: Access the data we collect about you
s8.right2: Request correction or updating of your data
s8.right3: Request deletion of your data in certain cases
s8.right4: Object to certain types of processing
s8.right5: Withdraw your consent at any time
s8.right6: Transfer your data to another party

s9.title: 9 — Cookies
s9.p: The platform uses cookies to improve performance, remember your preferences, personalize content, and measure performance. You may disable them from your browser settings, noting that this may affect some platform functions.

s10.title: 10 — Changes to This Policy
s10.p: This policy may be updated periodically. We will notify you of material changes via email or a clear notice within the platform. Continuing to use the platform after an update is published constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.subtitle: The rules governing use of the Makani Spot platform and the rights and duties of all parties
terms.intro: Makani Spot is a digital platform operated by Kiosq SpotHub and governed by Egyptian law. The platform specializes in managing, organizing, and leasing small commercial spaces, kiosks, bazaars, and temporary events. By using the platform, you expressly agree to all Terms and Conditions set out here.

s1.title: 1 — Definitions of Parties and Key Terms
s1.li1: "The Company" or "The Platform": Kiosq SpotHub, operator of the Makani Spot platform.
s1.li2: "User": any natural or legal person who uses the platform in any capacity.
s1.li3: "Space Owner": the person or entity owning or managing a commercial space listed on the platform.
s1.li4: "Tenant" or "Business Owner": anyone seeking a space for their business or project.
s1.li5: "The Service": all functions and features available within the platform.

s2.title: 2 — General Provisions
s2.li1: These Terms are governed by applicable Egyptian laws and regulations.
s2.li2: The platform reserves the right to modify or develop its services or policies at any time, notifying users of material changes.
s2.li3: The platform acts as an intermediary and organizer connecting parties, and is not a direct party to any contract concluded between users unless otherwise stated.
s2.li4: Users must be at least 18 years of age or hold a recognized legal capacity.

s3.title: 3 — User Obligations
s3.li1: Provide accurate, correct information and keep it continuously updated.
s3.li2: Keep login credentials confidential and not share them with others.
s3.li3: Bear full legal responsibility for all activities on the platform.
s3.li4: Comply with all Egyptian laws and regulations relevant to their business activity.
s3.li5: Pay any fees or commissions due by their specified deadlines.

s4.title: 4 — Prohibited Conduct (Strictly Forbidden)
s4.li1: Posting misleading information or falsified data about activities or spaces
s4.li2: Uploading content that violates Egyptian law or public decency
s4.li3: Engaging in any fraud, deception, or scams
s4.li4: Attempting to breach platform systems or tamper with data
s4.li5: Using circumvention tools (VPN, proxy) to bypass platform policies
s4.li6: Violating other users' privacy or misusing their data
s4.li7: Creating fake or multiple accounts for manipulative purposes

s5.title: 5 — Fees, Payments, and Refunds
s5.li1: The platform reserves the right to charge service fees, brokerage commissions, or listing and promotion fees.
s5.li2: All fees are subject to taxes and charges as prescribed by Egyptian law.
s5.li3: Fees paid are refundable only in accordance with the company's clearly published refund policy.
s5.li4: The platform is committed to transparently displaying all prices and fees before completing any transaction.

s6.title: 6 — Limitation of Liability
s6.p: The platform does not fully guarantee the accuracy of content posted by users and bears no responsibility for any losses or damages resulting from:
s6.li1: Incorrect information provided by other users
s6.li2: Temporary technical failures or service interruptions beyond the company's control
s6.li3: Business decisions users make based on information from the platform
s6.li4: Disputes arising directly between space owners and tenants

s7.title: 7 — Account Suspension and Service Termination
s7.p: The company reserves the right to suspend or terminate any user's account in the following cases:
s7.li1: A proven violation of these Terms and Conditions
s7.li3: Repeated, documented complaints from other users
s7.li4: A request from the competent judicial or regulatory authorities

s8.title: 8 — Dispute Resolution
s8.p: In the event of any dispute, the company is committed to first attempting an amicable resolution. If that is not possible, the dispute will be settled before the competent Egyptian courts under Egyptian law, without prejudice to either party's right to arbitration under the rules of the Cairo Regional Centre for International Commercial Arbitration.

terms.contactLabel: For inquiries about the Terms and Conditions:

ip.title: Intellectual Property Policy
ip.subtitle: Protecting the rights of the company and users under Egyptian Law No. 82 of 2002

s1.title: 1 — Platform Intellectual Property
s1.p: All of the following materials and elements are the exclusive property of Kiosq SpotHub, protected under Egyptian law and international intellectual property agreements:
s1.editorialValue: Text · Business guide · Studies and reports
s1.p2: Copying, distributing, reverse-engineering, or reusing any part of the platform without prior specific written consent from the company is strictly prohibited.

s2.title: 2 — User-Generated Content
s2.p: When uploading any content to the platform (photos, logos, designs, text), the user represents and warrants that:
s2.li1: They own full rights and licenses to the uploaded content.
s2.li2: The content does not infringe any third-party intellectual property rights.
s2.li3: They bear full legal responsibility for any claims arising from their content.

s3.title: 3 — License Granted to the Platform
s3.p: By publishing your content on the platform, you grant Kiosq SpotHub a non-exclusive, non-transferable, royalty-free license to:
s3.li1: Display and operate the content within the platform to provide the service.
s3.li2: Include it in the platform's marketing and promotional materials.
s3.li3: Archive it securely for backup and legal-compliance purposes.
s3.p2: Ownership of the content does not transfer to the company under this license, and the user remains the exclusive owner of their content.

s4.title: 4 — Reporting Intellectual Property Infringement
s4.p: If you believe your intellectual property rights have been infringed on the platform, please submit a formal report including:
s4.li1: A detailed description of the infringing content and its location on the platform.
s4.li2: Proof of your ownership of the original content (document, license, registration receipt).
s4.li4: A statement affirming the accuracy of the information in the report.
s4.step1: Report received with confirmation reply within 72 business hours.
s4.step2: Report and referenced content reviewed within 7 business days.
s4.step3: Appropriate action taken: removal, disabling, or further review.
s4.step4: All parties notified of the review outcome.

s5.title: 5 — Penalties and Legal Action
s5.p: The company reserves the right to take full legal action under Egyptian Intellectual Property Protection Law No. 82 of 2002 against any documented infringement, including claims for material and moral damages.
